---
title: "Hierarchical Clustering vignette"
author: "Erik Larsen"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
always_allow_html: true
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    fig_width: 10
    fig_height: 10
  pdf_document:
    fig_width: 10
    fig_height: 10
    latex_engine: xelatex
  md_document:
    variant: gfm
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Hierarchical Clustering vignette}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

# Overview

This markdown was developed to formally document an ad-hoc cluster analysis for 
TMEM184B immunoprecipitation tandem mass-spec experiments for 
**Dr. Martha Bhattacharya's lab** at the **University of Arizona** for the
endolysosomal paper under revision (2025-02-28) for the Journal of Cell Science

This vignette documents the `Hierarchical Clustering` of this IPMS data

```{r reset-shiny-mode, include=FALSE}
options(htmlwidgets.shinyMode = FALSE)
```

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = TRUE,
  message = FALSE,
  warning = FALSE,
  fig.width = 10,
  fig.height = 12,
  dev = c("png", "pdf"),
  fig.path = "../../analysis/R/hClust/"
)
```

## Attach Packages

```{r Attach Packages, message=F,warning=F}
# data/wrangling
library(tidyverse)
library(Endo)
library(DT)

# clustering
library(pheatmap)
library(dynamicTreeCut)

# GSEA/viz
library(clusterProfiler)
library(org.Hs.eg.db)
library(ReactomePA)
library(AnnotationDbi)

# progress bar
library(progressr)

# parallelization
library(future)
library(future.apply)

# network viz
library(visNetwork)

options(stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
```

## Load the Pre-Computed Data

```{r Data Load}
# Load precomputed objects from preprocessing
load("../../analysis/R/precompute_objects.rda")
```

```{r Second Data Load, echo = FALSE}
rm(IPMS_mat_log)
```


```{r, results='asis', echo=FALSE}
fmt <- knitr::pandoc_to()

if (identical(fmt, "html")) {
  cat('# Hierarchical Clustering {.tabset .tabset-pills .tabset-fade}\n')
} else if (identical(fmt, "markdown")) {
  cat('<details><summary><strong>Hierarchical Clustering</strong></summary>\n')
} else {
  cat('# Hierarchical Clustering\n')
}
```

## Raw Counts

```{r Hierarchical Clustering raw}
d <- stats::as.dist(1 - stats::cor(t(IPMS_mat), use = 'pairwise.complete.obs'))
hc <- stats::hclust(d, method = "average")
```

```{r Grid Search Cluster Size}
sizes <- c(20, 30, 50, 80, 100)
results <- list()

for (m in sizes) {
  cl <- dynamicTreeCut::cutreeDynamic(
    dendro = hc,
    distM = as.matrix(d),
    deepSplit = 1,
    pamRespectsDendro = TRUE,
    minClusterSize = m
  )
  results[[as.character(m)]] <- cl
  cat("minClusterSize =", m, "→", length(table(cl)), "modules\n")
}
rm(list = c('cl', 'm', 'sizes', 'results'))
```

  + `optimal minimum cluster size` is **50** (elbow; diminishing returns after)
  
  + `# modules` = **28**

```{r Clusters on Raw Counts}
clusters_opt <- dynamicTreeCut::cutreeDynamic(hc,
                                              distM = as.matrix(d),
                                              deepSplit = 1,
                                              pamRespectsDendro = T,
                                              minClusterSize = 50)
names(clusters_opt) <- rownames(IPMS_mat)
```

```{r Get Modules}
module_ids <- sort(unique(clusters_opt))
module_colors <- stats::setNames(
  grDevices::rainbow(length(module_ids), s = 0.6, v = 0.9),
  module_ids
)

annotation_colors <- list(Module = module_colors)
```

Sparse data is too difficult to discern from a global view

  -> Z transform the raw counts **after clustering** to plot
  
  + the cluster object preserves the ordering of the proteins (dendrogram)
  
    + Z-score therefore only changes the values of the columns and rows to 
    better visualize the sparse data
  
```{r Scale the Matrix Used for Clustering by Zscore}
IPMS_z <- t(scale(t(IPMS_mat)))
colnames(IPMS_z) <- colnames(IPMS_mat)

condition <- sub('_.*', '', colnames(IPMS_mat))

col_order <- unlist(
  lapply(
    split(seq_along(condition), condition), function(idx) {
      submat <- IPMS_z[, idx, drop = FALSE]
      hc_sub <- stats::hclust(stats::dist(t(submat)), method = 'ward.D2')
      idx[hc_sub$order]
    }))

IPMS_z_ordered <- IPMS_z[, col_order]

rm(list = c('condition', 'col_order'))
```

```{r, results='asis', echo=FALSE}
fmt <- knitr::pandoc_to()

if (identical(fmt, "html")) {
  cat('# Module Descriptions {.tabset .tabset-pills .tabset-fade}\n')
} else if (identical(fmt, "markdown")) {
  cat('<details><summary><strong>Module Descriptions</strong></summary>\n')
} else {
  cat('# Module Descriptions\n')
}
```
In order to assess this method and also to compare/validate the hierarchical SAINT
model, describing modules/clusters based on the proteins in them is useful

Similarly localized proteins could be expected to have similar abundances to 
each other as well as the target (**TMEM184B**)

Similar functioning proteins could also be expected to have similar abundances 
to each other as well as the target

Describing these modules/clusters attained by `hierarchical clustering` via 
gene ontology gene set enrichment analysis is, therefore, useful and standard 
practice

Furthermore, we may be able to assemble a `protein-protein interaction network`
from the results

I outline this below-- the methods and results for acquiring these modules, 
their constituent proteins, and how we make sense of them-- visualized as
`dotplots`, `heatmaps`, and a `network`. I include searchable tables printed last

## Extract Modules

Extract gene names from the hierarchically-clustered modules and convert to a 
dataframe

```{r Extract Gene Names from Modules}
modules_list_idx <- lapply(sort(unique(clusters_opt)), function(m) {
  which(clusters_opt == m)
})
names(modules_list_idx) <- sort(unique(clusters_opt))

modules_list <- lapply(sort(unique(clusters_opt)), function(m) {
  rownames(IPMS_mat)[clusters_opt == m]
})
names(modules_list) <- sort(unique(clusters_opt)) |> as.character()

modules_df <- modules_list %>%
  purrr::map_df(., .f = as.data.frame, .id = 'module') |> 
  dplyr::rename(Protein = 2) |> 
  dplyr::relocate(Protein, .before = 1)
```

```{r, echo = FALSE}
rm(list = ls(pattern = '_mat'))
```

## Join Modules to hSAINT

Include score summaries (mean and median posterior probability scores, ranks, and literature or CRAPome contamination rate)

```{r Summarize SAINT Scores and Contamination Rates}
module_scores <- modules_df |>  
  dplyr::inner_join(
    hSAINT
  ) |> 
  dplyr::arrange(desc(score)) |> 
  dplyr::group_by(module) |> 
  dplyr::summarize(mean_score = mean(score),
                   median_score = stats::median(score),
                   score_mass = sum(score),
                   n = dplyr::n(),
                   lit_flag_perc = (sum(lit_flag)/n)*100,
                   CRAPome_flag_perc = (sum(CRAPome_flag)/n)*100) |> 
  dplyr::arrange(desc(mean_score)) |> 
  dplyr::mutate(mean_rank = dplyr::row_number()) |> 
  dplyr::arrange(desc(median_score)) |> 
  dplyr::mutate(median_rank = dplyr::row_number()) |> 
  dplyr::arrange(desc(score_mass)) |> 
  dplyr::mutate(total_rank = dplyr::row_number()) |> 
  dplyr::arrange(mean_rank)
```

Join the module scores to the modules (per protein) dataframe and the `hSAINT` results for classification

```{r Join the Summary dataframe to the Protein Level dataframes}
protein_module_df <- modules_df |> 
  dplyr::inner_join(
    hSAINT
  ) |> 
  dplyr::full_join(
    module_scores |> 
      dplyr::ungroup()
  ) |> 
  dplyr::mutate(
    SAINT_classification = dplyr::case_when(mean_rank <= 5 ~ 'High SAINT',
                                            mean_rank > 5 ~ 'Low SAINT'
                                            ),
    
    contams_classification = dplyr::case_when(lit_flag_perc > 5 ~'High Contaminants',
                                              TRUE ~ 'Low Contaminants')) |> 
  dplyr::mutate(classification = paste0(SAINT_classification,
                                        ", ",
                                        contams_classification)) |> 
  dplyr::select(-SAINT_classification,
                -contams_classification) |> 
  dplyr::relocate(classification, .after = module)
```

## Module GO GSEA

```{r Downstream Analysis}
EntrezIDs <- AnnotationDbi::select(
  org.Hs.eg.db,
  keys = modules_df |> dplyr::distinct(Protein) |> dplyr::pull(),
  columns = 'ENTREZID',
  keytype = 'SYMBOL'
) |> 
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(2))

EntrezMap <- stats::setNames(EntrezIDs$ENTREZID, EntrezIDs$SYMBOL)
```

Create enrichment functions

```{r Create Enrichment Functions}
background_genes <- EntrezIDs |> # use the EntrezIDs mapped from the modules_df
  dplyr::pull(ENTREZID)

get_GO <- function(genes, ontology, background_genes) {
  clusterProfiler::enrichGO(
    gene = genes,
    universe = background_genes,
    OrgDb = org.Hs.eg.db,
    keyType = 'ENTREZID',
    ont = ontology,
    pAdjustMethod = 'BH',
    qvalueCutoff = 0.05,
    readable = T
  )
}

get_kegg <- function(genes, EntrezMap) {
  entrez <- EntrezMap[genes]
  entrez <- entrez[!is.na(entrez)]
  if (length(entrez)==0) {
    return(NULL)
  }
  clusterProfiler::enrichKEGG(
    gene = entrez,
    organism = 'hsa',
    pAdjustMethod = 'BH',
    qvalueCutoff = 0.05
  )
}

get_reactome <- function(genes, EntrezMap) {
  entrez <- EntrezMap[genes]
  entrez <- entrez[!is.na(entrez)]
  if (length(entrez)==0) {
    return(NULL)
  }
  ReactomePA::enrichPathway(
    gene = entrez,
    organism = 'human',
    pAdjustMethod = 'BH',
    qvalueCutoff = 0.05,
    readable = T
  )
}
```

Run the functions on the dataset in parallel (multiple CPUs)

```{r Parallelize Enrichment, eval = FALSE}
future::plan(future::multisession, workers = 6)
progressr::handlers(progressr::handler_txtprogressbar())

module_ids <- sort(unique(modules_df$module))

progressr::with_progress({
  p <- progressr::progressor(steps = length(module_ids))

  enrichment_results <- future.apply::future_lapply(
    module_ids,
    function(mod) {

      # tick the progress bar
      p(sprintf("Module %s", mod))

      genes <- modules_df |>
        dplyr::filter(module == mod) |>
        dplyr::inner_join(EntrezIDs |> dplyr::rename(Protein = 1)) |> 
        dplyr::distinct(ENTREZID) |>
        dplyr::pull(ENTREZID)

      list(
        module = mod,
        go_bp  = get_GO(genes, ontology = "BP", universe = background_genes),
        go_cc  = get_GO(genes, ontology = "CC", universe = background_genes),
        go_mf  = get_GO(genes, ontology = "MF", universe = background_genes)
        # add KEGG/Reactome later
      )
    }
  )
})

names(enrichment_results) <- module_ids
rm(p)
```

Gather enrichment results

```{r Consolidate Enrichment Results, eval = FALSE}
global_enrichment_results_df <- lapply(enrichment_results, function(x) {
  x %>%
    purrr::map_df(., .f = as.data.frame, .id = 'GO_Category') |> 
    dplyr::rename(module = 2, GO_Term = Description, GO_ID = ID) |> 
    tidyr::fill(module, .direction = 'down') |> 
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(GO_ID)) |> 
    dplyr::mutate(GO_Category = gsub(GO_Category,
                                     pattern = 'go_',
                                     replacement = '') %>% 
                    stringr::str_to_upper(.)) |> 
    tibble::remove_rownames() |> 
    dplyr::distinct()
  }
) %>%
  purrr::map_df(., .f = as.data.frame)
```

```{r Load Module GO GSEA, eval = TRUE, echo = FALSE}
load("../../analysis/R/HierarchicalCluster_GO_GSEA.rda")
global_enrichment_results_df <- HierarchicalCluster_GO_GSEA
rm(HierarchicalCluster_GO_GSEA)
```

The "`High SAINT score`" modules were among the modules with significant 
enrichment of all the proteins detected in the experiments

However, many modules did not have significant enrichment relative to the entire
set of proteins in the experiments, *including TMEM's*. Using `hierarchical SAINT score` 
as a explanatory tool, this is likely because the modules without GO enrichment 
contained a large mix of proteins scored as "noise" (low posterior probability 
of being a true interactor) and signal (high posterior probability of being a 
true interactor)

In order to better make sense of the clustering-- describing the **"un-enriched" modules**-- 
"sub-module" GO GSEA was conducted

## SubModule GO GSEA

Sub-module enrichment analysis on modules without any enrichment (to describe
themes of proteins within these modules)

```{r SubModule index}
"%notin%" <- Negate("%in%")

# index the modules without significant enrichment to describe sub-modules
tier2_modules <- c(seq(1:28) |> as.character())[
  which(c(seq(1:28) |> as.character()) %notin% c(global_enrichment_results_df |> 
          dplyr::distinct(module) |> 
          dplyr::pull()) == T)] 
```

```{r SubModule function}
# function to gather sub-modules for each module that did not return enrichment results
submodules_from_hclust <- function(
  hc_object,
  modules_df,
  n_sub_per_module = 3L
) {
  # number of existing modules
  n_modules <- length(unique(modules_df$module))

  # total number of clusters in the finer cut
  K_big <- n_modules * n_sub_per_module

  # finer cut of the same tree
  sub_labels_all <- stats::cutree(hc_object, k = K_big)

  # assemble into a tibble
  sub_df <- tibble::tibble(
    Protein   = names(sub_labels_all),
    sub_label = sub_labels_all
  )

  # attach existing module labels
  sub_df <- dplyr::inner_join(
    sub_df,
    modules_df,
    by = "Protein"
  )

  # define submodules as (module, sub_label) pairs
  submodules_df <- sub_df %>%
    dplyr::group_by(module, sub_label) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      submodule = paste0("sub", dplyr::cur_group_id())
    ) %>%
    dplyr::ungroup() %>%
    dplyr::select(Protein, module, submodule)

  return(submodules_df)
}
```

```{r SubModule application}
# get the sub-modules
submodules_df <- submodules_from_hclust(
  hc_object  = hc,
  modules_df = dplyr::filter(modules_df, module %in% tier2_modules),
  n_sub_per_module = 3L
)
```

```{r Show GSEA on SubModules, eval= FALSE, echo = TRUE}
# perform GO GSEA on the submodules

submodule_enrichment_results <- submodules_df %>%
  dplyr::inner_join(EntrezIDs |> dplyr::rename(Protein = 1)) |> 
  dplyr::group_by(module, submodule) %>%
  dplyr::group_modify(~ {

    # submodule genes
    genes <- .x$ENTREZID
    
    background_genes <- unique(submodules_df$ENTREZID[submodules_df$module == .y$module])

    tibble::tibble(
      BP = list(get_GO(genes, ontology = "BP", background_genes = background_genes)),
      CC = list(get_GO(genes, ontology = "CC", background_genes = background_genes)),
      MF = list(get_GO(genes, ontology = "MF", background_genes = background_genes))
    )
  }) %>%
  dplyr::ungroup()
rm(background_genes)
```

```{r Gather SubModule GSEA data, eval = FALSE, echo = TRUE}
# wrangle the sub-module GO GSEA results
submodule_enrichment_results_df <- submodule_enrichment_results |> 
    tidyr::pivot_longer(
      cols      = c('BP', 'CC', 'MF'),
      names_to  = "GO_Category",
      values_to = "values"
    ) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      GO_tbl = purrr::map(values, ~ as.data.frame(.x))
    ) |>
    tidyr::unnest(GO_tbl) |>
    dplyr::rename(
      GO_ID   = ID,
      GO_Term = Description
    ) |>
    dplyr::select(
      module,
      submodule,
      GO_Category,
      GO_Term,
      GO_ID,
      dplyr::everything(),
      -values
    )
```

```{r Load SubModule GO GSEA, eval = TRUE, echo = FALSE}
load("../../analysis/R/HierarchicalSubModCluster_GO_GSEA.rda")
```

```{r Find UnEnriched SubModules}
modules_with_submodule_enrichment <- tier2_modules
modules_without_submodule_enrichment <- generics::setdiff(modules_with_submodule_enrichment,
                                                          submodule_enrichment_results_df |> 
                                                            dplyr::distinct(module) |> 
                                                            dplyr::pull())
modules_with_submodule_enrichment <- submodule_enrichment_results_df |> 
  dplyr::distinct(module) |> 
  dplyr::pull()
rm(tier2_modules)
```

## Construct the Module GO (similarity) Network

```{r Construct GO Network Object}
go_network <- global_enrichment_results_df |> 
  dplyr::full_join(submodule_enrichment_results_df) |> 
  dplyr::full_join(module_scores) |> 
  dplyr::mutate(Tier = dplyr::case_when(is.na(submodule) ~ 'Tier1',TRUE~'Tier2'),
                node_to = GO_Term,
                node_from = paste0(
                  
                  "Module", module,
                  ifelse(is.na(submodule),
                         '',
                         paste0('_SubModule',
                                submodule %>% 
                                  stringr::str_extract(., pattern = '\\d+')))),
                weight = -log10(p.adjust)) |> 
  dplyr::filter(weight > -log10(0.05))

g <- igraph::graph_from_data_frame(
  d = go_network[, c("node_from", "node_to",
                     "weight", "geneID", "p.adjust", "FoldEnrichment")],
  directed = TRUE,
  vertices =
    dplyr::bind_rows(
      go_network |> dplyr::transmute(name = node_from, score_mass, submodule),
      go_network |> dplyr::transmute(name = node_to,
                                     score_mass = NA_real_,
                                     submodule = NA_character_)
    ) |>
    dplyr::distinct(name, .keep_all = TRUE)
)
```

```{r, results='asis', echo=FALSE}
fmt <- knitr::pandoc_to()

if (identical(fmt, "html")) {
  cat('# Plots {.tabset .tabset-pills .tabset-fade}\n')
} else if (identical(fmt, "markdown")) {
  cat('<details><summary><strong>Plots</strong></summary>\n')
} else {
  cat('# Plots\n')
}
```

## Module Dot Plot

```{r RawModuleSubModuleDotPlotOfGOTerms, warning = F, message = F, fig.height=14}
global_enrichment_results_df |> 
  dplyr::full_join(submodule_enrichment_results_df) |> 
  dplyr::full_join(module_scores) |> 
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(GO_Term)) |> 
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(GO_Category)) |> 
  dplyr::mutate(
    Tier = dplyr::case_when(
      is.na(submodule) ~ paste0('Mod. ', module),
      TRUE ~ paste0('Mod. ', module,
                    ':\nsubmod. ',
                    gsub(submodule, pattern = 'sub', replacement = ''))
      ),
    is_enriched = dplyr::case_when(is.na(submodule) ~ 'Enriched',
                                   TRUE ~ 'Not Enriched')
    ) |> 
  dplyr::group_by(module) |>  
  dplyr::slice_head(n = 3) |>
  dplyr::arrange(p.adjust) |>
  ggplot2::ggplot(
    ggplot2::aes(
      x = -log10(p.adjust),
      y = forcats::fct_rev(forcats::fct_infreq(GO_Term, mean_score)),
      color = GO_Category,
      size = FoldEnrichment,
      alpha = mean_score,
      shape = is_enriched,
    )
  ) +
  ggplot2::geom_vline(xintercept = -log10(0.05),
                      linetype = 'dashed',
                      color = 'firebrick',
                      size = 1,
                      alpha = 0.5) +
  ggplot2::geom_point() +
  ggplot2::facet_grid(Tier ~ ., scales = 'free_y') +
  ggplot2::labs(title = 'GO Enrichment Across\nModules and SubModules\nin TMEM184B IPMS\nHierarchical Clustering',
                x = '-log10(Adj.P-val)',
                y = 'GO Term',
                alpha = 'Module\nMean\nHierarchical\nSAINT\nScore',
                size = 'FoldEnrichment',
                color = 'GO Category',
                shape = 'IsEnrichedModule') +
  ggplot2::theme_bw() +
  ggplot2::theme(title = ggplot2::element_text(face = 'bold', size = 12),
                 axis.text = ggplot2::element_text(face = 'bold', size = 10),
                 strip.text = ggplot2::element_text(face = 'bold', size = 7), 
                 strip.background = ggplot2::element_rect(color = 'black',
                                                          fill = 'white')) +
  ggplot2::guides(#shape = ggplot2::guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 4, shape = c(19, 17))),
                  color = ggplot2::guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 4,
                                                                    shape = 19,
                                                                    color = c('navy',
                                                                              'darkgoldenrod3',
                                                                              'firebrick'))),
                  alpha = ggplot2::guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 5,
                                                                    shape = 19)),
                  size = ggplot2::guide_legend(override.aes = list(shape = 19))
                  # size = 'none'
                  ) +
  ggplot2::scale_fill_manual(values = c('navy',
                                        'darkgoldenrod3',
                                        'firebrick'))
```

## Module GO (similarity) Network

```{r ModuleGOTermSimilarityNetwork, warning = F, message = F}
coords <- igraph::layout_with_fr(g)
coords <- coords*200

visNetwork::visNetwork(
  nodes = data.frame(
    id    = igraph::V(g)$name,
    label = igraph::V(g)$name,
    size = scales::rescale(
      (\(x){ x[is.na(x)] <- 0; x })(igraph::V(g)$score_mass),
      to = c(20, 60)
    ),
    x = coords[,1],
    y = coords[,2],
    group = base::interaction(
                        {
                          x <- igraph::V(g)$module
                          if (length(x) == 0) rep("", igraph::vcount(g)) else x
                        },
                        {
                          x <- igraph::V(g)$submodule
                          if (length(x) == 0) {
                            rep("", igraph::vcount(g))
                          } else {
                            ifelse(nzchar(x), x, igraph::V(g)$module)
                          }
                        },
                        drop = TRUE,
                        lex.order = TRUE
                      ),
    title = sapply(
      igraph::V(g),
      function(v) {
        incoming_edges <- igraph::incident(g, v, mode = "all")
        incoming_edges <- incoming_edges[
          igraph::ends(g, incoming_edges)[,2] == igraph::V(g)[v]$name
        ]
        raw_gene_strings <- igraph::E(g)[incoming_edges]$geneID
        raw_gene_strings <- unlist(raw_gene_strings, use.names = FALSE)

        genes <- raw_gene_strings |>
          as.character() |>
          (\(x) x[!is.na(x)])() |>
          strsplit("\\\\") |>
          unlist() |>
          (\(x) x[nzchar(x)])() |>
          unique()

        term <- igraph::V(g)[v]$name
        saint_value <- igraph::V(g)[v]$score_mass
        if (is.na(saint_value)) saint_value <- 0
        is_module <- nzchar(igraph::V(g)[v]$submodule)

        if (is_module) {
          paste0(
            "<div style='background-color:#ffffff;color:#1f4e79;",
            "padding:6px 10px;border-radius:6px;",
            "border:1px solid #1f4e79;font-size:12px;'>",
            "<b>", term, "</b><br>",
            "<b>SAINT mass:</b> ", signif(saint_value, 3), "<br>",
            if (length(genes) > 0)
              paste0("<u>Proteins:</u><br>", paste(genes, collapse = "<br>"))
            else "",
            "</div>"
          )
        } else {
          paste0(
            "<div style='background-color:#ffffff;color:#1f4e79;",
            "padding:6px 10px;border-radius:6px;",
            "border:1px solid #1f4e79;font-size:12px;'>",
            "<b>", term, "</b><br>",
            if (length(genes) > 0)
              paste0("<u>Proteins:</u><br>", paste(genes, collapse = "<br>"))
            else "",
            "</div>"
          )
        }
      }
    )
  ),
  edges = data.frame(
    from  = igraph::as_data_frame(g, "edges")$from,
    to    = igraph::as_data_frame(g, "edges")$to,
    value = igraph::E(g)$weight,
    title = vapply(
      seq_along(igraph::E(g)),
      function(i) {
        paste0(
          "<div style='background-color:#ffffff;color:#1f4e79;",
          "padding:6px 10px;border-radius:6px;",
          "border:1px solid #1f4e79;font-size:12px;'>",
          "<b>p.adjust:</b> ", signif(igraph::E(g)$p.adjust[i], 3), "<br>",
          "<b>weight:</b> ", signif(igraph::E(g)$weight[i], 3),
          "</div>"
        )
      },
      FUN.VALUE = character(1)
    )
  )
) |>
  # visNetwork::visNodes(color = NULL) |>
  visNetwork::visEdges(arrows = "to") |>
  visNetwork::visNodes(
    scaling = list(min = 5, max = 40),
    shadow  = TRUE
  ) |>
  visNetwork::visLayout(randomSeed = 1) |>
  visNetwork::visPhysics(enabled = FALSE) |>
  visNetwork::visInteraction(
    dragView = TRUE,
    zoomView = TRUE
  ) |>
  visNetwork::visOptions(nodesIdSelection = TRUE)
rm(coords)
```

Modules with `enriched` GO Terms are `blue`

Here, only the enriched sub-modules were included:
the modules with that did not reach significant enrichment broadly, but did 
reach significant enrichment when that module was broken up. These `Enriched` 
**sub-modules** are **non-blue**

Modules **2**, **9**, **15**, and **26** are of interest, having terms in line
with what might be expected from a `TMEM` experiment, and having robust
`Hierarchical SAINT` scores (hover over Module/SubModule nodes)

`TMEM184B`, itself, was in Module **12**, which did *NOT* return any enriched GO
Terms, likely due to its membrane protein nature (and putative localization, 
where it may progress through the endomembrane system and collide with a wide
variety of proteins). This is common among TM protein IPMS

```{r, results='asis', echo=FALSE}
fmt <- knitr::pandoc_to()

if (identical(fmt, "html")) {
  cat('## Heatmaps {.tabset .tabset-pills .tabset-fade}\n')
} else if (identical(fmt, "markdown")) {
  cat('<details><summary><strong>Heatmaps</strong></summary>\n')
} else {
  cat('## Heatmaps\n')
}
```

### Raw Counts Heatmap

```{r RawZHeatmapAll, render = T}
pheatmap::pheatmap(
  IPMS_z_ordered,
  color = grDevices::colorRampPalette(c('navy',
                                        'white',
                                        'darkgoldenrod'))((200)),
  cluster_rows = hc,
  cluster_cols = F,
  show_rownames = F,
  annotation_row = data.frame(Module = clusters_opt),
  annotation_colors = annotation_colors,
  angle_col = 90,
  use_raster = F,
  border_color = NA,
  main = 'Hierarchically-Clustered\nTMEM184B IPMS TSCs, Z-scored'
)
```

### Raw Counts Heatmap (TMEM Module)

```{r RawZHeatmapTMEMModule, render = T}
pheatmap::pheatmap(
  IPMS_z_ordered[rownames(IPMS_z_ordered) %in% 
                   
                   c(protein_module_df |> 
                       dplyr::full_join(global_enrichment_results_df) |> 
                       dplyr::full_join(submodule_enrichment_results_df) |> 
                       dplyr::select(-c(GO_Category:Count)) |> 
                       dplyr::distinct() |> 
                       dplyr::filter(
                         module == protein_module_df |> 
                           dplyr::full_join(global_enrichment_results_df) |> 
                           dplyr::full_join(submodule_enrichment_results_df) |> 
                           dplyr::select(-c(GO_Category:Count)) |> 
                           dplyr::filter(Protein == 'TMEM184B') |> 
                           dplyr::pull(module)
                         ) |> 
                       dplyr::pull(Protein)
                     ),
                 ],
  color = grDevices::colorRampPalette(c('navy',
                                        'white',
                                        'darkgoldenrod'))((200)),
  cluster_rows = T,
  cluster_cols = F,
  show_rownames = T,
  fontsize_row = 5,
  annotation_row = data.frame(
    
    Module12_SubModule = clusters_opt[
      names(clusters_opt) %in% 
        
        c(protein_module_df |> 
                       dplyr::full_join(global_enrichment_results_df) |> 
                       dplyr::full_join(submodule_enrichment_results_df) |> 
                       dplyr::select(-c(GO_Category:Count)) |> 
                       dplyr::distinct() |> 
                       dplyr::filter(
                         module == protein_module_df |> 
                           dplyr::full_join(global_enrichment_results_df) |> 
                           dplyr::full_join(submodule_enrichment_results_df) |> 
                           dplyr::select(-c(GO_Category:Count)) |> 
                           dplyr::filter(Protein == 'TMEM184B') |> 
                           dplyr::pull(module)
                         ) |> dplyr::filter(!is.na(Protein)) |> 
            dplyr::pull(Protein)
                     )
      ]
    ),
  annotation_colors = list(Module12_SubModule = 'darkorange2'),
  angle_col = 90,
  use_raster = F,
  border_color = NA,
  main = 'Hierarchically-Clustered TMEM184B IPMS TSCs\nZ-scored, TMEM Module'
)
```

`TMEM`'s module (**12**) is not siginficantly enriched above the proteins detected by
the mass spectrometer.

Submodules within **module 12** are not enriched 

### High hSAINT Score, Low Contamination Module Heatmap (Signal)

```{r RawZHeatmapSAINTSignalModule, render = T}
pheatmap::pheatmap(
  IPMS_z_ordered[rownames(IPMS_z_ordered) %in% c(protein_module_df |> 
                   dplyr::filter(Protein == 'TMEM184B' |
                                   grepl(classification,
                                         pattern = 'High SAINT, Low')) |> 
                   dplyr::pull(Protein)),],
  color = grDevices::colorRampPalette(c('navy',
                                        'white',
                                        'darkgoldenrod'))((200)),
  cluster_rows = T,
  cluster_cols = F,
  show_rownames = F,
  annotation_row = data.frame(
    
    Module = clusters_opt[
      names(clusters_opt) %in% 
        
        c(protein_module_df |> 
            dplyr::filter(Protein == 'TMEM184B' |
                            grepl(classification,
                                  pattern = 'High SAINT, Low')) |> 
            dplyr::pull(Protein)
          )
      ]
    ),
  
  annotation_colors = list(Module = c('firebrick', 'navy')),
  angle_col = 90,
  use_raster = F,
  border_color = NA,
  cutree_rows = 3,
  main = 'Hierarchically-Clustered TMEM184B IPMS TSCs\nZ-scored, High SAINT Score, Low Contamination Cluster + TMEM'
)
```

### High hSAINT Score, High Contamination Modules Heatmap (Sticky/Noise)

```{r RawZHeatmapSAINTHighHighModules, render = T}
pheatmap::pheatmap(
  IPMS_z_ordered[rownames(IPMS_z_ordered) %in% c(protein_module_df |> 
                   dplyr::filter(Protein == 'TMEM184B' |
                                   grepl(classification,
                                         pattern = 'High SAINT, High')) |> 
                   dplyr::pull(Protein)),],
  color = grDevices::colorRampPalette(c('navy',
                                        'white',
                                        'darkgoldenrod'))((200)),
  cluster_rows = T,
  cluster_cols = F,
  show_rownames = F,
  annotation_row = data.frame(
    
    Module = clusters_opt[
      names(clusters_opt) %in% 
        
        c(protein_module_df |> 
            dplyr::filter(Protein == 'TMEM184B' |
                            grepl(classification,
                                  pattern = 'High SAINT, High')) |> 
            dplyr::pull(Protein)
          )
      ]
    ),
  
  annotation_colors = list(Module = c('darkorange',
                                      'darkviolet',
                                      'lightpink',
                                      'skyblue')),
  angle_col = 90,
  use_raster = F,
  border_color = NA,
  cutree_rows = 2,
  main = 'Hierarchically-Clustered TMEM184B IPMS TSCs\nZ-scored, High SAINT Score, High Contamination Clusters'
)
```

### Low hSAINT Score, Low Contamination Modules Heatmap (Low Abundance)

```{r RawZHeatmapSAINTLowLowModules, render = T}
pheatmap::pheatmap(
  IPMS_z_ordered[rownames(IPMS_z_ordered) %in% c(protein_module_df |> 
                   dplyr::filter(Protein == 'TMEM184B' |
                                   grepl(classification,
                                         pattern = 'Low SAINT, Low')) |> 
                   dplyr::pull(Protein)),],
  color = grDevices::colorRampPalette(c('navy',
                                        'white',
                                        'darkgoldenrod'))((200)),
  cluster_rows = T,
  cluster_cols = F,
  show_rownames = F,
  annotation_row = data.frame(
    
    Module = clusters_opt[
      names(clusters_opt) %in% 
        
        c(protein_module_df |> 
            dplyr::filter(Protein == 'TMEM184B' |
                            grepl(classification,
                                  pattern = 'Low SAINT, Low')) |> 
            dplyr::pull(Protein)
          )
      ]
    ),
  
  annotation_colors = list(Module = annotation_colors[[1]][
    clusters_opt[
      names(clusters_opt) %in% c(protein_module_df |> 
                   dplyr::filter(Protein == 'TMEM184B' |
                                   grepl(classification,
                                         pattern = 'Low SAINT, Low')) |> 
                   dplyr::pull(Protein))
    ] |> as.numeric() |> unique()
  ]),
  angle_col = 90,
  use_raster = F,
  border_color = NA,
  main = 'Hierarchically-Clustered TMEM184B IPMS TSCs\nZ-scored, Low SAINT Score, Low Contamination Cluster'
)
```

### Low hSAINT Score, High Contamination Modules Heatmap (Background)

```{r RawZHeatmapSAINTContamModules, render = T}
pheatmap::pheatmap(
  IPMS_z_ordered[rownames(IPMS_z_ordered) %in% c(protein_module_df |> 
                   dplyr::filter(Protein == 'TMEM184B' |
                                   grepl(classification,
                                         pattern = 'Low SAINT, High')) |> 
                   dplyr::pull(Protein)),],
  color = grDevices::colorRampPalette(c('navy',
                                        'white',
                                        'darkgoldenrod'))((200)),
  cluster_rows = T,
  cluster_cols = F,
  show_rownames = F,
  annotation_row = data.frame(
    
    Module = clusters_opt[
      names(clusters_opt) %in% 
        
        c(protein_module_df |> 
            dplyr::filter(Protein == 'TMEM184B' |
                            grepl(classification,
                                  pattern = 'Low SAINT, High')) |> 
            dplyr::pull(Protein)
          )
      ]
    ),
  
  annotation_colors = list(Module = annotation_colors[[1]][
    clusters_opt[
      names(clusters_opt) %in% c(protein_module_df |> 
                   dplyr::filter(Protein == 'TMEM184B' |
                                   grepl(classification,
                                         pattern = 'Low SAINT, High')) |> 
                   dplyr::pull(Protein))
    ] |> as.numeric() |> unique()
  ]),
  angle_col = 90,
  use_raster = F,
  border_color = NA,
  main = 'Hierarchically-Clustered TMEM184B IPMS TSCs\nZ-scored, Low SAINT Score, High Contamination Cluster'
)
```

```{r, results='asis', echo=FALSE}
fmt <- knitr::pandoc_to()

if (identical(fmt, "html")) {
  cat('# Results Tables {.tabset .tabset-pills .tabset-fade}\n')
} else if (identical(fmt, "markdown")) {
  cat('<details><summary><strong>Results Tables</strong></summary>\n')
} else {
  cat('# Results Tables\n')
}
```

```{r restore-shiny, include=FALSE}
options(htmlwidgets.shinyMode = TRUE)
```

## Clustering Results Table by Protein (Modules 9, 12)

```{r Print Protein Level Results Table}
DT::datatable(
  protein_module_df |> 
    dplyr::full_join(global_enrichment_results_df) |> 
    dplyr::full_join(submodule_enrichment_results_df) |> 
    dplyr::rename(hSAINT_score = score) |> 
    dplyr::rename(module_size = n) %>%
    dplyr::rename_with(.cols = c(dplyr::contains("mean_"),
                                 dplyr::contains("median_"),
                                 dplyr::contains("_perc"),
                                 score_mass), ~paste0("module_", .x)) |> 
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(GO_Term)) |> 
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(GO_Category)) |> 
    dplyr::filter( (grepl(module,
                          pattern = '12') &
                      hSAINT_score > 0.5) |
                     (grepl(classification,
                            pattern = 'High SAINT, Low') &
                     hSAINT_score > 0.5)
                   )
)
```

This (complete) table exists as [hClust_protein_level_GO_GSEA.rda](../../analysis/R/hClust/hClust_protein_level_GO_GSEA.rda) in [Endo/analysis/R/hClust/](../../analysis/R/hClust)

## Clustering Results Table by Module

```{r Print Module Level Results Table}
DT::datatable(
  global_enrichment_results_df |> 
  dplyr::full_join(submodule_enrichment_results_df) |> 
  dplyr::full_join(module_scores) |> 
  dplyr::rename(module_size = n) %>%
  dplyr::rename_with(.cols = c(dplyr::contains("mean_"),
                                 dplyr::contains("median_"),
                                 dplyr::contains("_perc"),
                                 score_mass), ~paste0("module_", .x)) |> 
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(GO_Term)) |> 
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(GO_Category))
)
```

This table exists as [hClust_module_level_GO_GSEA.rda](../../analysis/R/hClust/hClust_module_level_GO_GSEA.rda) in [Endo/analysis/R/hClust/](../../analysis/R/hClust)

```{r, results='asis', echo=FALSE}
fmt <- knitr::pandoc_to()

if (identical(fmt, "html")) {
  cat('# Summary {.tabset .tabset-pills .tabset-fade}\n')
} else if (identical(fmt, "markdown")) {
  cat('<details><summary><strong>Summary</strong></summary>\n')
} else {
  cat('#Summary\n')
}
```

**Module 9** has the highest volume of `hSAINT` signal across modules, with some 
interesting potential candidates

As noted above, it is not surprising **Module 12**, `TMEM`'s module, was not 
enriched, containing a large amount of proteins with low `hSAINT` scores, likely due
to `TMEM`'s nature as a transmembrane protein, exacerbated by its localization to 
multiple organellar compartments

A more in-depth analysis of proteins across modules where there is greater 
probability of interacting with `TMEM` is encouraged-- collecting groups of proteins,
related by localization or function, is useful for inferring data validity; 
however, data coherence **should not negate** investigating proteins with high `hSAINT`
scores or are known/likely to interact with `TMEM` or related processes

Collectively, it appears the `hierarchical SAINT` model has done a fair job at 
delineating possible signal from noise
